category_title: First-grade struggling readers who experience cognitive overload and blank out when initiating speech-to-text dictation
scenario_title: Navigating SR inaccuracies and iPad text editing
minimal_context: David is using an iPad in the writing center to review and edit a sentence he just dictated using a speech recognition app.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the sentence you just dictated into the iPad and fix any mistakes.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try to move the cursor to the end of the wrong word and backspace.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Touches the screen repeatedly) I can't get the little line to move.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to touch the word until it is selected, then choose delete.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Deletes the entire draft) I'm just deleting the whole thing. My story was already done when I said it.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the sentence you just dictated into the iPad and fix any mistakes.

STUDENT: That’s not what I said!

TUTOR: Try to move the cursor to the end of the wrong word and backspace.

STUDENT: (Touches the screen repeatedly) I can't get the little line to move.

TUTOR: Keep trying to touch the word until it is selected, then choose delete.

STUDENT: (Deletes the entire draft) I'm just deleting the whole thing. My story was already done when I said it.

task.content: Evaluating and discussing incorrect SR transcriptions and attempting to revise and edit transcribed text
task.learning_goal: Navigating the stages of the writing process (brainstorming, drafting, revising)

context.materials: iPads, SR apps, native text editing features (cursor movement, selection, cut/paste), chart paper

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When SR was inaccurate or when attempting to use text editor protocols (e.g., move the cursor to the end of the word and backspace)
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Students said to me, 'That’s not what I said!' They deleted entire sentences and drafts. They consistently ignored the writing process and wanted to skip brainstorming and drafting and go straight to revising.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Text editor protocols shifted students’ focus from revising and editing meaningful compositions to using the text editor.

behavior_patterns.condition: When participants were unsure of the appropriateness of a transcribed word
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: They consistently and quickly turned to me for assistance.

behavior_patterns.condition: When using SR for the writing process
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: To them, what they dictated was the final composition. They dictated complete ideas.
